'''Benjamin Bolger''' (born 13 September 1989) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who currently plays for [[Abu Dhabi Harlequins]] in the [[Emirates Rugby League]] competition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.rlef.eu.com/news/article/631/uae-aims-for-the-stars |title=RLEF |access-date=2013-06-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714145439/http://www.rlef.eu.com/news/article/631/uae-aims-for-the-stars |archive-date=2014-07-14 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.thenationalnews.com/sport/former-london-bronco-ben-bolger-is-happy-to-teach-rugby-and-compete-1.654755 |title=Former London Bronco Ben Bolger is happy to teach rugby and compete | the National |access-date=20 June 2013 |archive-date=13 May 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140513043928/http://www.thenational.ae/sport/uae-sport/former-london-bronco-ben-bolger-is-happy-to-teach-rugby-and-compete |url-status=live }}</ref> He previously played for [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]]. His position of preference is {{rlp|SR}}.

Born in [[Chingford]], East London, Bolger started his rugby league career as a youth and an amateur player for [[Rugby League Conference South Premier|Conference South]] side [[St Albans Centurions]], starting as a 13-year-old, as well as playing for [[Saracens F.C.]], Chingford RFC, and representing the county of Essex, in [[rugby union]]. He signed for the Harlequins RL Under-16s after the St. Albans Centurions and was their academy player of the year for 2009. After signing professionally for the Harlequins RL at the start of 2010's [[Super League XV]], Bolger made his début for the Harlequins RL (later London Broncos) in the season opener against [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], coming off the [[Rugby league positions#Substitutes|bench]].<ref name="Bolger Poised For Quins Début">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/10/01/29/manual_074215.html|title=Bolger Poised For Quins Début|publisher=sportinglife.com|date=2010-01-30|accessdate=2010-01-30|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110604214857/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague%2F10%2F01%2F29%2Fmanual_074215.html|archivedate=2011-06-04}}</ref><ref>http://www.league.quins.co.uk/rugby/11115.php{{dead link|date=October 2016|bot=InternetArchiveBot|fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

Along with his younger brother Sam, Bolger moved to the [[United Arab Emirates]], and joined the Abu Dhabi Harlequins club in 2013, immediately becoming captain of the side and leading them in their inaugural rugby league campaign.{{citation needed|date=March 2020}}
